candidates.comparison: HuggingFace greedy vs vLLM greedy (with variants: prompt_embeds, async_scheduling, model_executor)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — exact string and token ID equality via check_outputs_equal
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: yes — HF and vLLM use different attention backends, memory layouts, and kernel implementations; FP differences can cause token divergence even with greedy decoding
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: yes — no Strong Contract covers cross-runtime (HF vs vLLM) comparison; enable_prompt_embeds=True variant also falls under Not Strong #9

candidates.comparison: HuggingFace single-GPU greedy vs vLLM TP=2 greedy
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — exact string and token ID equality via check_outputs_equal
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: yes — tensor parallelism introduces all-reduce operations, different GEMM partitioning, and different attention kernel paths; numeric differences are expected
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: yes — Not Strong #4: tensor parallel exact output equality is not a strong contract; also cross-runtime (HF vs vLLM) comparison has no listed Strong Contract
